repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md PartialID placeholder: Manski-family and Balke-Pearl-style bounds expected for pid_* entries; pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2: sharp dynamic IV LP projections

published_prior_art.cite: Manski1990: nonparametric treatment-effect bounds optimize over compatible latent distributions
AtheyChettyImbensKang2019: surrogate-index ATE identification under Prentice-style surrogacy/comparability

repo_prior_art.cite: q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends: aggregate pretrend summaries lose target-relevant latent information; pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2: dynamic IV LP projection, neither is noisy long-term surrogate drift partial ID

published_prior_art.cite: FanManzanaresParkQi2026 gives unknown-copula surrogate-index identified sets; HuangWangYuanZhaoZhang2026 gives longitudinal surrogate/pivot point extrapolation, neither gives noisy-panel drift tightening
ChenGengJia2007/VanderWeele2013/MaYinLiuGeng2021 address surrogate-paradox criteria; BibautKallusEjdemyrZhao2023 uses many weak experiments/proxy moments, not single-panel drift transport endpoints

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a short comparison to LalImbensHull2026 and, if retained, clarify that their many-noisy-proxy latent-surrogate model is an estimation/bias-variance result rather than a sharp drift-aware partial-ID result.

Make the definition of Gamma_d(P;rho) fully explicit by spelling out the bridge-compatible column-moment equations; this will reduce ambiguity in the sign-boundary conjecture without changing the kernel.
Add a minimal numerical binary-panel witness for Conjecture 1 showing terminal-only endpoint removal at rho below kappa_b(P); this would make the field-level novelty easier to audit.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0 with Conjecture 1 as the primary kernel and Conjecture 2 as the sign-transport companion, after tightening the Gamma_d notation.
